subject: GRB 250202A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 01:08:03 UT on 2 Feb 2025, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 250202A (trigger 760151288.464456 / 250202047).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 295.3, Dec = -35.0 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 19h 41m, -35d 00'), with a statistical uncertainty of 9.0 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 36.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250202047/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn250202047.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250202047/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn250202047.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250202047/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn250202047.gif
